extracted.brandBackground: Hydrogen Fitness offers luxury fitness facilities featuring fitness classes, free weights, cardio equipment, and a hydro bar.

leadership.name: Andrew Pinon
leadership.background: CEO of Change My Body LLC since 2012.
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings over a decade of fitness industry leadership experience.

leadership.name: Jonathan Gutwein
leadership.background: President of affiliate EGOpharma LLC since 2003.
leadership.whyItMatters: Provides extensive executive management and pharmaceutical/supplement industry experience.

item19.notes: Figures represent historical results of affiliate-owned outlets during the 2025 measurement period, adjusted to reflect franchisee royalty and brand fund fees.

lineItems.notes: Estimated cost for a 10,000 to 40,000 square foot space.
Includes security deposit and three months of rent.
Exterior and interior brand identification signage.
Free weights, cardio equipment, and other gym gear.
POS system, computer hardware, and software.
Pro-rated cost of insurance over a three-month period.

lineItems.category: Travel and Living Expenses While Training
lineItems.notes: Expenses for up to three people.
Minimum required spend for initial launch.

hiddenCosts.name: Technology Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Covers software licensing, maintenance, intranet, and website access.

hiddenCosts.name: Annual Convention Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Fee per attendee if a franchisee convention is organized.

hiddenCosts.name: Alternative Supplier Evaluation Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Reimbursement of costs to evaluate unapproved products or suppliers.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by mediation, arbitration, or litigation only in Connecticut.

operationalRisks.title: Personal Guaranty Required
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee and all owners must sign a personal guarantee.

operationalRisks.title: Short Operating History
operationalRisks.description: The franchisor is at an early stage of development with limited operating history.

extracted.conceptRationale: Hydrogen Fitness operates luxury fitness facilities with classes and gym equipment.
extracted.staffingRationale: Requires a Key Manager and fitness trainers to operate classes and manage the facility.

riskReasons: Capital gap of $1,643,750 is ~6.6x your liquid — heavy reliance on outside financing.
A high-severity operational tripwire is disclosed — see the tripwires section.
Franchisee unit economics could not be scored — no usable Item 19 (company/affiliate-owned only, or no FPR). Treat this as UNVERIFIED, not low-risk.
notes: No Item 19 middle-cohort or network-average revenue found; economics are indeterminate.

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $1,893,750 mid-point build-out, there is a $1,643,750 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal.

insights.conceptRationale: Hydrogen Fitness operates luxury fitness facilities with classes and gym equipment.

benchmark.label: Boutique fitness / studio
benchmark.dominantRisk: Member retention/churn and fixed-cost coverage — NOT cost of goods. The deal is decided by recurring membership against rent and equipment lease.
benchmark.rampNote: 9–18 months to a mature, retained member base.
considerations: COGS is trivial; the risks are member churn and covering fixed rent + equipment lease.
Ask Item 20 operators for monthly retention, average membership lifetime, and break-even member count.
Model CAC and free-trial conversion — member acquisition cost is the hidden operating line.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 15–28% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Boutique fitness / studio” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor flagged its own financial condition as a risk. The figures we extracted look comparatively stable, so confirm directly against the most recent statements.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
